text:
WHOLE BODY FDG PET-CT WITH CONCURRENT MRI BRAIN (GADOLINIUM)

CLINICAL INDICATION: 56-year-old female with metastatic melanoma (BRAF V600E-mutant, Stage IV M1c), originally presenting with bilateral nodal, subcutaneous, and hepatic involvement. History of prior BRAF/MEK inhibitor therapy (Tafinlar/tram) with response then progression at 8 months. Subsequently treated with ipi/Opdivo combination immunotherapy (3 cycles, complicated by Grade 3 immune-mediated colitis) followed by Opdivo monotherapy maintenance for approximately 23 months. Most recent CT approximately 6 months prior demonstrated near-complete response with a single residual sub-centimeter hepatic lesion. PET-CT requested to assess for complete metabolic response and guide discussion regarding treatment discontinuation. Concurrent MRI brain for intracranial reassessment. LDH (day of scan): 131 U/L.

---

TECHNIQUE:
Whole body FDG PET-CT was performed following intravenous administration of 10.3 mCi F-18 fluorodeoxyglucose. Blood glucose at time of injection: 88 mg/dL. Patient fasted for 6 hours prior to tracer administration. Uptake period: 65 minutes. Emission data acquired from vertex to mid-thigh in 3D mode. Concurrent CT for attenuation correction and anatomic correlation performed without intravenous contrast from skull base through pelvis (low-dose technique). Reconstructed images reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. Standard uptake value (SUV) calculations performed.

MRI brain performed with gadolinium (gadobutrol 0.1 mmol/kg IV). Sequences: T1, T2, T2 FLAIR, DWI, pre- and post-contrast T1 in axial/coronal/sagittal planes.

---

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK:
No FDG-avid cervical, submandibular, or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. The right level II cervical lymph node previously measuring up to 2.2 cm at peak progression — and most recently 0.3 cm on prior CT — is not resolved to a discrete node on the current low-dose CT. No abnormal FDG uptake in the head and neck region. No parotid, thyroid, or salivary gland uptake above background. Thyroid gland is heterogeneous in texture on CT, consistent with known treated hypothyroidism, without focal hypermetabolic activity.

CHEST / AXILLAE:
Pulmonary parenchyma: No nodules, masses, or areas of consolidation identified. No pleural effusions. No FDG uptake above background in the lung fields. No evidence of immune-related pneumonitis (no ground-glass or reticulated changes).

Mediastinum and hilar regions: No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes. Mediastinal structures are within normal limits. No hypermetabolic activity in the mediastinum.

Axillary regions: The previously bulky right axillary lymphadenopathy — which had measured 4.2 cm with an SUVmax of 18.4 at initial staging and subsequently decreased to 0.5-0.6 cm on maintenance scans — now shows complete resolution of any discrete lymph node mass. Small sub-centimeter lymph nodes in bilateral axillae are within normal size range and demonstrate no FDG uptake above expected background. No left axillary FDG-avid adenopathy.

Soft tissues / subcutaneous: No FDG-avid subcutaneous or cutaneous nodules identified. Previously extensive subcutaneous tumor deposits on the trunk and bilateral upper extremities have shown progressive resolution over the treatment course; no residual hypermetabolic foci identified on the current study. Skin and subcutaneous soft tissues appear unremarkable.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with smooth margins. CT attenuation is homogeneous. A 0.3 cm hypodense focus is again seen in segment 6, essentially unchanged in size from the most recent prior CT. Critically, this lesion demonstrates no FDG uptake above background hepatic parenchymal activity (SUVmax of this focus is less than surrounding liver background, estimated 1.4 vs. background 2.1). This metabolic profile is not consistent with viable tumor; the appearance is most consistent with a fibrotic scar or small cyst. The remaining hepatic parenchyma shows no additional focal lesions or suspicious hypermetabolic foci. No hepatic FDG-avid disease identified. Prior hepatic metastases, which numbered 6 at initial staging with the largest measuring 2.4 cm and SUVmax 11.8, have shown complete metabolic resolution.

Spleen: Normal in size, no focal lesions, no FDG avidity above background spleen activity.

Pancreas: Normal. No pancreatic lesions. No pancreatic duct dilation.

Adrenal glands: Bilateral adrenal glands are within normal limits. No adrenal nodules or masses. No FDG-avid adrenal activity.

Kidneys: Normal in size and contour bilaterally. Normal excretion of tracer into the collecting systems. No hydronephrosis. No FDG-avid renal lesions.

Retroperitoneal and mesenteric lymph nodes: No enlarged or FDG-avid lymphadenopathy. No retroperitoneal or mesenteric nodal disease identified.

Peritoneum / omentum: No FDG-avid peritoneal disease. The previously identified peritoneal nodule adjacent to the falciform ligament (0.9 cm at detection, not visualized on CT for several cycles) continues to show no identifiable discrete lesion and no abnormal peritoneal FDG uptake. No ascites.

Pelvis: No pelvic lymphadenopathy. No pelvic soft tissue masses. Uterus and adnexae unremarkable. Urinary bladder is partially filled with expected urinary tracer excretion. No inguinal FDG-avid adenopathy. Bilateral inguinal lymph nodes within normal size range without hypermetabolic activity. This is notable given that bilateral inguinal nodal disease was present at initial staging (right 2.1 cm SUVmax 7.6, left 1.4 cm SUVmax 5.3).

BONE AND MARROW:
No FDG-avid osseous lesions identified. No lytic or sclerotic bony lesions on CT. Bone marrow activity is within expected physiologic range. No evidence of marrow infiltration.

OTHER:
No FDG-avid lesions identified in the remaining visualized soft tissues. No incidental findings of clinical concern.

---

MRI BRAIN WITH GADOLINIUM:

Brain parenchyma: No focal parenchymal signal abnormalities. No areas of restricted diffusion to suggest acute or subacute infarction. No areas of T2/FLAIR signal change to suggest white matter disease or vasogenic edema.

Post-contrast sequences: No enhancing parenchymal lesions. No leptomeningeal enhancement. No pachymeningeal enhancement. No evidence of intracranial metastatic disease.

Ventricles and sulci: Normal ventricular size and configuration. Sulcal and cisternal spaces appropriate for age.

Cranial nerves: Cranial nerve enhancement not identified.

Posterior fossa: Cerebellum and brainstem unremarkable. No posterior fossa lesions.

Orbit and skull base: No orbital masses. Paranasal sinuses demonstrate mild mucosal thickening in the maxillary sinuses bilaterally, likely reflecting chronic sinusitis — unchanged from prior. No skull base lesions.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No FDG-avid disease identified anywhere in the body. The study is notable for the complete absence of hypermetabolic activity at all previously involved sites, including bilateral axillary and inguinal nodal regions, hepatic parenchyma, and subcutaneous/cutaneous soft tissues.

2. The residual 0.3 cm segment 6 hepatic hypodense focus is metabolically silent with SUVmax below background hepatic parenchymal activity. This is most consistent with a fibrotic scar or small benign cyst rather than residual viable tumor. No other hepatic lesions are visualized.

3. No intracranial metastases on gadolinium-enhanced MRI brain. The brain appears clear.

4. No evidence of immune-related lung toxicity or other treatment-related parenchymal changes.

5. Overall, the imaging findings represent a complete metabolic response. All previously documented sites of disease — hepatic lesions initially numbering 6 with SUVmax up to 11.8, bilateral axillary adenopathy with an index node peak SUVmax of 18.4, inguinal adenopathy, subcutaneous nodules, and peritoneal implant — are no longer metabolically active. The extent of response achieved is remarkable given the initial tumor burden at presentation.
